titleStmt.title: Memorandum by the Assistant Chief of the Division of Lend-Lease and Surplus War Property (Maxwell)

div.head: Memorandum by the Assistant Chief of the Division of Lend-Lease and Surplus War Property (Maxwell)
div.p: The Soviet Protocol Committee on August 17 sent out orders to the effect that loadings of Soviet ships in United States ports were to be stopped at once. Loadings of United States ships were also to be stopped and their cargoes discharged. In short, so far as practicable, transfers of lend-lease cargo to the U.S.S.R. were halted at the ports on August 17.

With respect to other lend-lease countries transfers are to be stopped only as of V–J Day.The signing of surrender by Japan took place September 2; for documentation on the Japanese surrender, see vol. vi , section under Japan entitled “Surrender of Japan …”, Part I. The outcome is, therefore, that the U.S.S.R. is being treated on a different basis from other countries.
When I inquired from John HazardSecretary of the President’s Soviet Protocol Committee. about this, he informed me that the decision was taken deliberately and probably was part of a general squeeze now being put on the U.S.S.R. Certain reasons can, however, be alleged for the discrimination, such as the fact that the U.S.S.R. does not provide us with reverse lend-lease and the fact that the U.S.S.R. does not assist us with redeployment.
